extraction.one_line_summary: BERNAL Egan attacked inside 3km to go on Gstaad summit finish, distancing DENNIS Rohan by 23 seconds before crucial TT.
tactical_patterns: BERNAL attacked with only 3km remaining on summit finish, late enough to prevent organized chase from Dennis support.
Bahrain-Merida used POZZOVIVO as decoy attacker then had him drop back to pace DENNIS, two-rider defensive tactic.
Movistar set tempo through SWIFT Ben before final kilometers, allowing BERNAL to attack fresh off team pace.
MAS Enric bridged ahead briefly before BERNAL's decisive move, testing GC group before team leader's attack.

rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Attacked late (3km) on summit finish to gain crucial time buffer before weak TT discipline against specialist.
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: negative: Lost 23 seconds despite teammate POZZOVIVO pacing, concerning defensive position before his strongest TT stage.
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: neutral: Deployed as initial attacker then domestique for DENNIS, tactical flexibility at veteran age.
rider_signals.FRANK Mathias: positive: Caught breakaway inside 10km on home climb, local knowledge enabling late-race strength.
rider_signals.KONRAD Patrick: neutral: Fourth place at 23 seconds, finishing with main GC group behind podium.
rider_signals.HIRT Jan: neutral: Fifth place in GC group, maintaining position without notable move.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Briefly ahead before final 3km, aggressive positioning suggests form despite no result.
